So first one that's near and dear to my heart is your email service provider, this is your email list host, this is where you keep your list of subscribers and then when you want to send an email to them you actually will go into this tool, put your email in and it will send out to everyone and that's how I can send out an email to a hundred thousand or two hundred thousand people all at once. Now I personally really prize stableness and continuity, I don't like bouncing around between email providers and I want that one that's just really strong and stable, I'm willing to sacrifice on other things like fancy features or ease of use which just doesn't isn't to say that the ones I've used are not easy to use but I'm just saying the number one thing for me is stable and something I can stick with

## [03:03 - 06:04]

and deliverability is the absolute number one thing with email providers that means can they get your email into people's inbox and not have it filtered out as spam. So deliverability is absolutely paramount, that's the most important thing and it does unfortunately tend to be a moving target, some hosts have great deliverability and then they'll go downhill so it's it is a moving target but some hosts are better than others. Second factor is speed and by this I mean how fast can they get your emails out and this really more applies once your list gets larger. When you first start out it's easy for a list of 500 people or a thousand people or a 5,000 people or 10,000 people it's easy to get that email out pretty quick but if you have a 50,000 or 70,000 or a hundred thousand some hosts might get that email out a lot slower and so deliverability is number one speed is next then after that comes the features are the next most powerful and being able to segment your lists because you will want to end up treating different groups differently like the number one most important is buyers versus non-buyers. Once you start your business you're gonna have people joining your list as prospects and then some of them buying from you and you have a fundamentally different relationship with those two groups and you need to be able to send different emails to the different groups and then also being able to track people by action and by interest so if they're clicking through on emails about a certain topic then it's great to give those people more emails about that topic and so being able to segment your lists or create multiple lists is powerful and most all of the email service providers offer this functionality one way or another so what I've used what I use for many many many years is a Weber and we didn't outgrow a Weber until just a year or two ago and that was because we moved to infusion soft which is had a lot more capabilities that had the email delivery built into the shopping cart and being able to integrate those two once we got advanced enough that became something that we really really wanted to do so we currently use infusion soft and then some of our broadcast emails are bigger broadcast emails we use a service called Mara post m a r o p o s t and they integrate with infusion soft and they're good for getting out the large broadcast so we use infusion soft for like automated follow-ups after maybe someone purchases from us or after they first join our

you want and we I personally in my business we have gotten pretty fancy we're using pro level DSLRs but that's just it's been a progression for us and like I said I still will release videos that I shoot on my phone if you want to go deep on video we have a program that was created by my son Dan who does all of our video all of my video and he is absolutely amazing he created a program called easy video awesomeness and as I record this it's not available at the minute but by the time you listen to this it might be version 2.0 so send an email to support at product launch formula that's support at product launch formula.com and ask them about EVA which is easy video awesomeness going forward for video editing there's lots of great tools I use Adobe Premiere which is actually a pro level tool but I find that easier to use than a lot of the beginner tools and I do very little video editing Dan does 99% of it but then every now and then I'll put out a simple video that I do myself and I use Adobe Premiere. Sony Vegas movie studio is a great intro level video editor for Windows iMovie is included on all the Macs so that's beginner level Camtasia is available for Windows and the Mac that's a great program and ScreenFlow is available for the Mac those are all video creation video editing programs and then if you want to create physical products like CDs DVDs books anything like that use disk.com and I've been using disk.com since oh boy 2005 it's been a long time so those are some of the tools one of the things is these tools keep evolving new tools keep on coming up this this portion of the course I guarantee you will be sports parts of it will become outmoded quickly but what you need to remember is that you just have to get started you just have to pick a tool pick a few tools and get started many of these are there could be three or four different choices and you could sit there and spin your wheels trying to figure out what the perfect solution is and just remember there is no perfect solution and then in terms of getting the stuff working pretty much all the vendors have great tutorials and these days their support's getting pretty darn good and if you're still stuck check out YouTube or Google and see if there's some other tutorials that are available again number one thing is don't get don't get into that paralysis analysis or analysis paralysis whichever it is where you just sit there and try to make the perfect decision
